1.title: Solomon's request
1.text: God appeared to Solomon by night at Gibeon and said, 'Ask what I shall give thee.' Solomon asked not for riches or long life, but for wisdom and knowledge to govern the people.

1.question: What was at Gibeon, leading Solomon to sacrifice there?
options: The tabernacle of the congregation and the bronze altar

1.question: What did Solomon ask of God at Gibeon?
options: Wisdom and knowledge to govern the people

1.question: What did God give Solomon beyond what he asked?
options: Riches, wealth, and honor like no king had

1.question: What marked Solomon's reign according to the chapter's end?
options: Accumulation of chariots, horses, and great wealth

2.title: A great house
2.text: Solomon said the house would be great, 'for great is our God above all gods.' Even the heavens cannot contain him; the temple would only be a place to burn incense before him.

2.question: Whom did Solomon ask for help in building the temple?
What did Solomon ask of Huram besides timber?
options: A skilled craftsman to work in gold, silver, and bronze

2.question: Whom did Solomon assign to the heavy labor of the temple?
options: The foreigners dwelling in the land

3.title: Mount Moriah
3.text: Solomon built the temple on Mount Moriah in Jerusalem, in the place David had prepared, on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite — the same mount of ancient sacred memory.

3.question: How did Solomon overlay the inside of the temple?
options: He covered it with fine gold and cypress

3.question: What did Solomon make for the most holy place?
options: Two cherubim of carved work, overlaid with gold

3.question: What names did Solomon give the two pillars before the temple?

4.title: The molten sea
4.text: Huram made a great bronze 'sea,' round, resting on twelve oxen — three facing each direction. It was for the priests to wash in.

4.question: On what did the great molten sea rest?
How many golden lampstands did Solomon put in the temple?
Who made the bronze furnishings of the temple?
Of what material were the finest furnishings of the temple?

5.title: The glory fills the house
5.text: When the musicians and singers praised as one voice — 'for he is good, for his mercy endureth for ever' — a cloud filled the house of the Lord, and the priests could not minister because of God's glory.

5.question: What did the priests and Levites bring into the temple?
options: The ark of the covenant and the holy vessels

5.question: Where was the ark placed in the temple?
options: In the most holy place, under the wings of the cherubim

5.question: Why could the priests not minister in the temple?
options: Because the glory of the Lord, as a cloud, filled the house

6.title: There is no God like thee
6.text: Solomon prayed: 'Lord God of Israel, there is no God like thee in the heaven, nor in the earth; which keepest covenant, and shewest mercy unto thy servants, that walk before thee with all their hearts.'

6.question: What did God say he had not chosen before choosing David?
options: A city or man to be prince over Israel

6.question: Who built the temple, fulfilling the word given to David?
What did Solomon acknowledge God had fulfilled to David?
options: The promise spoken by his mouth and fulfilled by his hand

6.question: What did Solomon ask God to do when hearing the prayers?
options: To hear from heaven and forgive

6.question: What did Solomon ask for when the people sinned and were defeated?
options: That on turning back, God would forgive and bring them home

6.question: Why did Solomon ask God's forgiveness for the people?
options: That they might fear him and walk in his ways

6.question: What did Solomon ask when the people went to war praying toward the temple?
options: That God hear their prayer from heaven and maintain their cause

6.question: How did Solomon end his prayer?
options: Asking God to remember the mercies promised to David

7.title: If my people humble themselves
7.text: God said: 'If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.'

7.question: What happened when Solomon finished praying?
options: Fire came down from heaven and consumed the sacrifices

7.question: How did the Lord appear to Solomon after the feast?
options: By night, saying he had heard his prayer

7.question: What did God promise Solomon if he walked like David?
options: To establish his throne over Israel

7.question: What would be the reason for ruin, if Israel forsook God?
options: For having left the Lord and served other gods

8.title: Care for the holy
8.text: Solomon moved Pharaoh's daughter out of the City of David, saying she should not dwell where the ark had entered, for those places were holy.

8.question: What did Solomon build besides the temple and palace?
options: Cities, including Tadmor in the wilderness

8.question: Whom did Solomon NOT enslave in his work?
By what did Solomon organize the service of priests and Levites?
options: By the order of David his father
By the custom of the pagans

8.question: From where did Solomon and Huram's fleets bring gold?

9.title: The half was not told me
9.text: The queen of Sheba, seeing Solomon's wisdom and wealth, said she had not believed the reports until she saw with her own eyes — and that not even half had been told her.

9.question: Why did the queen of Sheba come to Solomon?
options: To test him with hard questions

9.question: What did Solomon do with the algum wood brought from Ophir?
options: Steps for the temple and palace, and harps and psalteries

9.question: Of what material was Solomon's great throne?
What did Solomon's fleets bring on their return?
options: Gold, silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks

9.question: Over which kings did Solomon rule?
options: Over all kings from the Euphrates to Egypt

9.question: What happened at the end of Solomon's reign?
options: He died, and Rehoboam his son reigned in his place

10.title: The rejected counsel
10.text: Rehoboam forsook the counsel of the elders, who asked him to ease the people's yoke, and followed the counsel of the young men who grew up with him — a decision that split the kingdom.

10.question: What did the people ask of Rehoboam at Shechem?
options: To ease the heavy yoke his father had laid on them

10.question: What did the young men advise Rehoboam to answer?
options: That he would make the yoke even heavier
That he would ease the yoke
That he would consult a prophet

10.question: Why did Rehoboam not heed the people?
options: Because this turn was from God, fulfilling the word to Jeroboam

10.question: What was the result of Rehoboam's harsh answer?
options: Israel (the ten tribes) rebelled against the house of David

11.title: Do not fight
11.text: The prophet Shemaiah told Rehoboam not to go up to war against his brethren of Israel, for that division came from God — and the people obeyed and returned home.

11.question: Why did Rehoboam give up fighting against Israel?
options: Because the word of the Lord, by Shemaiah, forbade it

11.question: What did Rehoboam do to defend the kingdom of Judah?
options: He fortified cities and stocked them with provisions

11.question: What worship did Jeroboam set up in the north, driving the Levites to flee?
Who moved to Judah, strengthening Rehoboam's kingdom?
options: The priests, Levites, and God-fearing of Israel

11.question: How did Rehoboam act wisely regarding his many sons?
options: He dispersed them through the fortified cities of Judah
He gave them the northern kingdom

12.title: When they humbled themselves
12.text: Seeing that the king and princes humbled themselves, the Lord told Shemaiah he would not utterly destroy them but grant some deliverance — though they would still serve Egypt.

12.question: Why did Shishak of Egypt come up against Judah?
options: Because Rehoboam and the people forsook the law of the Lord

12.question: What did Shishak take when he plundered Jerusalem?
options: The temple and palace treasures, and the golden shields

12.question: How did Rehoboam's reign end?
options: He died and his son Abijah reigned in his place

13.title: God is with us
13.text: Abijah cried to Israel: 'Behold, God himself is with us for our captain, and his priests with sounding trumpets to cry alarm against you. O children of Israel, fight ye not against the Lord God of your fathers; for ye shall not prosper.'

13.question: What did Abijah proclaim on Mount Zemaraim?
options: That the kingdom of David was given by a covenant of salt to his sons
That he would forsake the temple

13.question: What worship did Abijah accuse Israel of following?
options: The golden calves Jeroboam had made for gods

13.question: How did Jeroboam try to surprise Judah's army?
options: He set an ambush behind them

13.question: Why did Judah prevail over Israel?
options: Because they relied on the Lord God of their fathers

13.question: Where were Abijah's acts written?
options: In the commentary of the prophet Iddo

14.title: It is nothing for thee
14.text: Asa cried: 'Lord, it is nothing with thee to help, whether with many, or with them that have no power; help us, O Lord our God, for we rest on thee.' And the Lord struck the Ethiopians.

14.question: What did Asa command Judah to do at the start of his reign?
options: To seek the Lord and keep the law and commandment

14.question: Who came against Asa with an army of a million men?
What happened to the Ethiopians before Judah?
options: They were struck and could not recover

15.title: If ye seek him
15.text: Azariah said to Asa: 'The Lord is with you, while ye be with him; and if ye seek him, he will be found of you; but if ye forsake him, he will forsake you.'

15.question: What happened to Israel when, in distress, they turned to the Lord?
options: He let himself be found by them

15.question: What did Asa do with the altar of the Lord?
options: He renewed it, before the porch of the temple

15.question: How did the people seal the covenant to seek God?
options: They swore to the Lord with shouting, trumpets, and cornets

15.question: What marked the early years of Asa's reign after the reform?
options: There was no war until the thirty-fifth year

16.title: The eyes of the Lord
16.text: Hanani said to Asa: 'The eyes of the Lord run to and fro throughout the whole earth, to shew himself strong in the behalf of them whose heart is perfect toward him. Herein thou hast done foolishly; therefore from henceforth thou shalt have wars.'

16.question: In whom did Asa trust against Baasha of Israel?
Why did Hanani rebuke Asa?
options: Because he leaned on Syria and not on the Lord

16.question: What did Asa do when severely diseased in his feet?
options: He sought the physicians, and not the Lord

17.title: Teaching the law
17.text: Jehoshaphat sent Levites and priests who went through all the cities of Judah carrying the book of the law of the Lord, teaching the people — a distinctive mark of his reign.

17.question: Why was the Lord with Jehoshaphat at first?
options: Because he walked in the first ways of David and sought not the Baalim

17.question: What effect did Jehoshaphat's faithful reign have on neighboring kingdoms?
options: The fear of the Lord fell on them, and they made no war

17.question: What characterized Jehoshaphat's army?
options: It was numerous and organized under mighty captains

17.question: Who served the king besides those set in the fortified cities?
options: Men of war stationed in Jerusalem

18.title: What my God saith
18.text: Pressed to prophesy in Ahab's favor, Micaiah answered: 'As the Lord liveth, even what my God saith, that will I speak' — and announced defeat, though it cost him prison.

18.question: What did Ahab's prophets say about the war for Ramoth-gilead?
options: To go up, for God would deliver it into the king's hand

18.question: What sign did Zedekiah make to promise Ahab victory?
options: Iron horns, saying with them he would push Syria

18.question: What did Micaiah finally prophesy to Ahab?
options: The defeat of Israel and the death of the king

18.question: What vision did Micaiah report of the heavenly court?
options: A spirit that would deceive Ahab through the prophets' mouths

18.question: What did Micaiah tell Zedekiah, who had struck him?
options: That he would see the truth the day he hid in a chamber

18.question: How did Ahab enter battle to escape the prophecy?
options: In disguise, while Jehoshaphat wore royal robes

18.question: How did Ahab die, fulfilling Micaiah's prophecy?
options: Struck by an arrow shot at random

19.title: Ye judge for the Lord
19.text: Jehoshaphat said to the judges: 'Take heed what ye do: for ye judge not for man, but for the Lord, who is with you in the judgment.'

19.question: Why did the seer Jehu rebuke Jehoshaphat?
options: For helping wicked Ahab and loving those who hate the Lord

19.question: What did Jehoshaphat charge the judges he appointed?
options: To act faithfully and with a perfect heart, in the fear of the Lord

20.title: Our eyes are upon thee
20.text: Jehoshaphat prayed before all Judah: 'O our God... we have no might against this great company that cometh against us; neither know we what to do: but our eyes are upon thee.'

20.question: What did Jehoshaphat do on learning of the great multitude coming against Judah?
options: He feared, sought the Lord, and proclaimed a fast throughout Judah

20.question: What did Jehoshaphat remind God of in his prayer?
options: That these peoples came to drive Judah from the inheritance God gave them

20.question: What did the Levite Jahaziel announce to the people?
options: That the battle was not theirs, but God's

20.question: What did Jehoshaphat place before the army?
options: Singers to praise the beauty of holiness

20.question: What did the people do on the fourth day, after the victory?
options: They gathered in the valley of Berachah to bless the Lord

20.question: How was Jehoshaphat's reign assessed?
options: He walked in the way of Asa, doing right in the Lord's eyes

20.question: Why were Jehoshaphat's ships broken?
options: For having allied with Ahaziah, the wicked king of Israel

21.title: For David's sake
21.text: Despite Jehoram's wickedness, the Lord would not destroy the house of David, because of the covenant he had made with David, promising to give him and his sons a light forever.

21.question: What did Jehoram do when established in the kingdom?
options: He killed all his brothers with the sword

21.question: What happened with Edom in Jehoram's reign?
options: It rebelled and Judah lost dominion over it

21.question: What did Elijah's letter announce to Jehoram?
options: A great plague on the people and disease in his bowels

21.question: How did Jehoram die and was he buried?
options: Of a bowel disease, undesired and without a king's honors

22.title: The hidden child
22.text: Jehoshabeath hid the baby Joash, son of Ahaziah, from among the king's sons who were to be slain, keeping him in the temple for six years — so the house of David was not wiped out under Athaliah.

22.question: Who counseled Ahaziah to act wickedly?
options: His mother Athaliah, of Ahab's house

22.question: What did Athaliah do on learning of her son Ahaziah's death?
options: She destroyed all the royal seed of Judah to reign

23.title: God save the king!
23.text: Jehoiada and his sons brought out the king's son, put the crown and the testimony on him, anointed him, and shouted: 'God save the king!' — restoring David's line to the throne.

23.question: Whom did Jehoiada gather to crown Joash safely?
options: The Levites, priests, and heads of the fathers' houses

23.question: How did the people react on seeing the king crowned?
options: They rejoiced and blew trumpets; Athaliah cried 'Treason!'

23.question: What covenant did Jehoiada make after crowning Joash?
options: That he, the people, and the king would be the Lord's people

23.question: What happened to the city after Athaliah's death?

24.title: The Lord look on it
24.text: As he was stoned by Joash's order, Zechariah son of Jehoiada said as he died: 'The Lord look upon it, and require it' — the king had forgotten the good Jehoiada had done him.

24.question: What did Joash resolve to do to the temple?
How was the money for the work gathered?
options: In a chest set at the gate of the house of the Lord

24.question: What happened while Jehoiada lived?
options: Burnt offerings were made continually in the house of the Lord

24.question: What did God send to bring Judah back to him?
options: Prophets, but the people would not hear them

24.question: What did Syria's army do to Judah at the end of Joash's reign?
options: It struck Judah and Joash, destroying the princes

24.question: How did Joash die?
options: His own servants slew him, for the blood of Zechariah

25.title: The Lord can give more
25.text: When Amaziah lamented the hundred talents paid to the mercenaries he had dismissed, the man of God said: 'The Lord is able to give thee much more than this' — for victory comes from God, not money.

25.question: What principle of the law did Amaziah follow in punishing his father's killers?
options: That fathers not die for children, nor children for fathers

25.question: Why did Amaziah dismiss the mercenaries of Israel?
options: A man of God warned the Lord was not with them

25.question: What sin did Amaziah commit after defeating Edom?
options: He brought back Edom's gods and worshiped them

25.question: Why did Amaziah not heed Joash's warning against war?
options: Because it was from God, to deliver them for seeking Edom's gods

25.question: What did Joash take from Jerusalem after defeating Amaziah?
options: Gold, silver, temple vessels, and hostages

25.question: How did Amaziah's reign end?
options: He was slain in a conspiracy and buried in Jerusalem

26.title: When he was strong
26.text: 'But when he was strong, his heart was lifted up to his destruction: for he transgressed against the Lord his God, and went into the temple of the Lord to burn incense upon the altar' — a task reserved for the priests.

26.question: What happened while Uzziah sought the Lord?
What shows that Uzziah loved farming?
options: He dug wells and had farmers and vinedressers

26.question: How did Uzziah equip his army?
options: With shields, spears, helmets, breastplates, bows, and slings

26.question: Why did the priests resist Uzziah in the temple?
options: Because only the priests could burn incense to the Lord

26.question: How did Uzziah end his days?
options: A leper and isolated, buried apart from the kings

27.title: He ordered his ways
27.text: 'So Jotham became mighty, because he prepared his ways before the Lord his God' — the secret of his power was not military, but faithfulness.

27.question: How did Jotham differ from his father Uzziah?
options: He did not enter the temple of the Lord

27.question: Why did Jotham become mighty?
options: Because he ordered his ways before the Lord

28.title: In distress, he sinned more
28.text: 'In the time of his distress did he trespass yet more against the Lord: this is that king Ahaz' — instead of humbling himself, he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus that had struck him.

28.question: What abomination did Ahaz commit, copying the pagans?
options: He burned his children in the fire

28.question: What did the prophet Oded demand of Israel concerning Judah's captives?
options: That they return them, for they too were guilty before God

28.question: What did the men of Israel do with Judah's captives?
options: They clothed, fed, and brought the feeble back to Jericho

28.question: What did Assyria do for Ahaz, who had called it?
options: Instead of helping, it distressed him

28.question: How was Ahaz buried?
options: In the city, but not in the sepulchres of the kings of Israel

29.title: Opening the doors
29.text: In the very first month of his first year, Hezekiah opened the doors of the house of the Lord, which Ahaz had shut, and repaired them — restoring the interrupted worship.

29.question: How did Hezekiah describe the fathers' sin to the Levites?
options: That they forsook the Lord and turned their backs on his tabernacle

29.question: What did Hezekiah purpose in his heart to do?
options: Make a covenant with the Lord, to turn away God's fierce wrath

29.question: What did the priests and Levites do with the temple's uncleanness?
options: They carried it out to the brook Kidron
They burned it on the altar

29.question: What was offered in the cleansing of the temple and kingdom?
options: Burnt offerings and a sin offering for Judah

29.question: How did Hezekiah arrange the music in worship?
options: With Levites, cymbals, psalteries, and harps, by David's order

29.question: How did the people respond to the restoration of worship?
options: They brought many burnt offerings with joy

29.question: Why did Hezekiah and the people rejoice?
options: Because God had prepared the people, for it was done suddenly

30.title: Turn again unto him
30.text: Hezekiah's invitation said: 'turn again unto the Lord... for the Lord your God is gracious and merciful, and will not turn away his face from you, if ye return unto him.'

30.question: What feast did Hezekiah invite all Israel to keep in Jerusalem?
How did many in the north react to Hezekiah's messengers?
What did the gathered people do before the Passover?
options: They removed the altars in Jerusalem and cast them into the Kidron

30.question: Why did Hezekiah pray for those who ate the Passover unclean?
options: That God pardon everyone who prepared his heart to seek him

30.question: What did the assembly decide after the seven days of feast?
options: To keep another seven days with joy

30.question: What did the priests and Levites do at the feast's end?
options: They blessed the people, and their prayer reached heaven
They dismissed the people in silence

31.title: With all his heart
31.text: 'And in every work that he began in the service of the house of God, and in the law, and in the commandments, to seek his God, he did it with all his heart, and prospered' — the summary of Hezekiah's reign.

31.question: What did the people bring in abundance when Hezekiah gave the command?
options: The firstfruits and tithes of everything

31.question: What did the high priest say about the heaped-up offerings?
options: That they ate, were satisfied, and plenty was left over

31.question: Why did the priests and Levites receive their portion?
options: So they might devote themselves to the service of the Lord's house

31.question: Why did Hezekiah prosper in all things?
options: Because he did everything with all his heart, seeking his God

32.title: With us is the Lord
32.text: Hezekiah encouraged the people: 'With him is an arm of flesh; but with us is the Lord our God to help us, and to fight our battles' — and the people rested on these words.

32.question: What did Hezekiah do to weaken the Assyrian siege?
options: He stopped the water springs outside the city

32.question: How did Sennacherib try to dishearten Judah?
options: He mocked, saying no god had saved its people from Assyria

32.question: Against whom did Sennacherib's servants speak blasphemies?
options: Against the Lord God and against Hezekiah

32.question: What happened after the angel of the Lord struck the Assyrians?
options: Many brought gifts to the Lord and to Hezekiah

32.question: How did Hezekiah respond, in the end, to the pride of his heart?
options: He humbled himself, with Jerusalem, and wrath did not come in his days

32.question: How was Hezekiah buried?
options: With honors, in the chief of the sepulchres of the sons of David

33.title: And he humbled himself
33.text: Imprisoned in Babylon, Manasseh prayed and humbled himself greatly before the God of his fathers; God was entreated, heard his supplication, and brought him back to Jerusalem — 'then Manasseh knew that the Lord he was God.'

33.question: Where did Manasseh dare to build altars to other gods?
options: In the two courts of the house of the Lord

33.question: How did Manasseh and the people respond to the Lord's warning?
What did Manasseh do after returning repentant from Babylon?
options: He repaired the Lord's altar and commanded Judah to serve the Lord

33.question: How did Amon's short reign end?
options: The people of the land slew the conspirators and made Josiah king

34.title: He tore his clothes
34.text: When he heard the words of the Book of the Law found in the temple, Josiah tore his clothes, for he saw how great was God's wrath because his fathers had not kept the word of the Lord.

34.question: What did Josiah do to the bones of the idolatrous priests?
options: He burned them on their altars, cleansing Judah
He took them to the temple

34.question: What was the money gathered in the temple used for?
options: To pay the workers who repaired the house of the Lord

34.question: What did Hilkiah the priest find in the temple?
options: The Book of the Law of the Lord, given by Moses

34.question: Why did Josiah send to inquire of the Lord about the Book?
options: Because great was God's wrath, for the fathers had not kept the word

34.question: Why did Huldah say Josiah would be spared the judgment?
options: Because his heart was tender and he humbled himself before God

34.question: What did Josiah do after renewing the covenant?
options: He removed the abominations from all Israel's lands and made the people serve the Lord

35.title: None since Samuel
35.text: 'There was no passover like to that kept in Israel from the days of Samuel the prophet' — Josiah's Passover surpassed in faithfulness all those of the kings before him.

35.question: How did Josiah organize the Levites for the Passover?
options: By courses, according to the fathers' houses, as David had written

35.question: What was prepared for the keeping of the Passover?
options: The service, with priests and Levites in their places

35.question: Who sang while the Passover was offered?
options: The singers, the sons of Asaph, in their place

35.question: What warning did Necho give Josiah before the battle?
options: That God had sent him, and Josiah should not oppose God

35.question: How did Josiah die?
options: Wounded by arrows in battle against Necho, at Megiddo

36.title: The land enjoyed her sabbaths
36.text: The exile lasted seventy years, 'to fulfil the word of the Lord by the mouth of Jeremiah, until the land had enjoyed her sabbaths' — resting all the time of the desolation.

36.question: What was Jehoiakim's reign in Judah like?
options: He did evil in the eyes of the Lord

36.question: What did Nebuchadnezzar take when he exiled Jehoiachin?
options: Jehoiachin and the precious vessels of the house of the Lord

36.question: Why did the Lord send his messengers to Judah?
options: Because he had compassion on his people and his dwelling place

36.question: What did the Babylonians do to Jerusalem?
options: They burned God's house, broke down the wall, and destroyed its palaces

36.question: What did Cyrus, king of Persia, decree at the end of the book?
options: That the house of the Lord be rebuilt in Jerusalem and the people go up
